title: <name> and the Case of the Talking Paintbrush

pages.text: It was Art Day at Rainbow School, and <name> was excited. She had her colors, paper, and favorite paintbrush — an old, fuzzy one with a blue handle. 'Today I’ll paint the best picture ever!' she said proudly.

But as she dipped the brush in yellow, a tiny voice squeaked, 'Not yellow again! Let’s try pink!' <name> jumped. 'Who said that?' 'It’s me!' said the brush. 'I’m tired of circles and suns. Let’s paint something fun — like a flying elephant!'

<name> blinked. 'Paintbrushes don’t talk!' 'Well, this one does,' grumbled the brush. 'Now pass the pink paint!' Before <name> could answer, the brush wiggled in her hand — swish-swoosh-splatter! Pink paint flew everywhere — on her paper, on her desk, even on the cat, Tinku, who yowled and ran leaving pink pawprints!

'Stop that!' <name> giggled. 'You’re painting my face!' 'Hmm, needs more sparkle!' said the brush, dipping into glitter paint. Splish! Splosh! Now the wall had rainbow spots, and <name>’s hair shone gold.

When the teacher came by, she gasped. '<name>! What is going on?' <name> blushed. 'Um… artistic creativity?' The teacher looked at her painting — a dragon with polka dots, an elephant wearing sunglasses, and a cat with pink whiskers. 'Well,' she said slowly, 'it’s… unusual.'

At the end of the day, the principal announced, 'The award for Most Creative Painting goes to <name>!' The class clapped. <name> grinned proudly — until she heard a whisper from her paintbox. 'See?' said the brush smugly. 'Told you pink was better.'

<name> rolled her eyes and whispered back, 'Next time, you’re painting the cleanup!' The brush chuckled, 'Deal! But only if we add glitter!' And that’s how <name> and her talking paintbrush became the most colorful (and sparkly) team in Rainbow School.
